recommended_primary_definition: Mean within-prompt population SD: S = J^-1 sum_j sqrt(n_j^-1 sum_k (x_jk - xbar_j)^2).

metric_roles.selectable_spread: Mean within-prompt population SD over the candidate set the judge actually sees. Use whole-pool candidates for selector accounting and host-only candidates for generator dynamics.
metric_roles.additive_variance_companion: Mean within-prompt population variance. Use for exact within/between-prompt and within/between-source decomposition; do not silently relabel it as spread.
metric_roles.total_distributional_breadth: Population SD after including between-prompt mean differences. This is often easier to predict from the generated mean, but it is not variation a within-prompt selector can rank.

binary_variance_geometry.identity: For binary candidate scores, own within-prompt variance = q(1-q) - variance_across_prompt_means.

all_binary_risk.model: Predict q_next from training displacement; persist between-prompt mean variance; set within-prompt variance to q_next(1-q_next) minus that component. For mean SD, persist its Jensen gap from RMS SD.

binary_risk_self_only.model: Predict q_next from training displacement; persist between-prompt mean variance; set within-prompt variance to q_next(1-q_next) minus that component. For mean SD, persist its Jensen gap from RMS SD.

binary_risk_mixed.model: Predict q_next from training displacement; persist between-prompt mean variance; set within-prompt variance to q_next(1-q_next) minus that component. For mean SD, persist its Jensen gap from RMS SD.
